        blogPost: `In today's digital age, social media has become an integral part of our lives, and Instagram, with its visually captivating platform, stands out as a popular choice for content creators looking to start a blog. Whether you're passionate about fashion, travel, food, fitness, or any other niche, Instagram offers an excellent opportunity to showcase your creativity and engage with a global audience. So, if you're eager to share your passions with the world, here's a step-by-step guide on how to start an Instagram blog and make it a resounding success.

1. Define Your Niche:
Before diving headfirst into Instagram blogging, it's crucial to identify your niche. Determine what interests you the most and where your expertise lies. A well-defined niche helps you stand out in a sea of content creators and attracts a dedicated audience genuinely interested in your content.

2. Create a Compelling Instagram Handle:
Your Instagram handle is essentially your brand name, so make it catchy and reflective of your niche. Keep it simple, memorable, and easy to spell to ensure that potential followers can find and remember you easily.

3. Develop a Cohesive Aesthetic:
Instagram is a visual platform, and maintaining a consistent and attractive aesthetic is key to grabbing attention and retaining followers. Choose a color palette, editing style, and overall theme that aligns with your niche and reflects your personality.

4. Craft an Engaging Bio:
Your Instagram bio is your chance to make a great first impression. Use it to concisely describe who you are, what your blog is about, and what value you provide to your followers. Include relevant keywords and a clear call-to-action (CTA) to encourage visitors to follow you.

5. Share High-Quality Content:
Quality is paramount on Instagram. Invest in a good camera or use your smartphone's advanced camera features to capture stunning images. Ensure your photos are well-lit, well-composed, and visually appealing. Additionally, use engaging captions that provide context, ask questions, or encourage interaction.

6. Plan Your Content Strategy:
Consistency is key to Instagram success. Plan your content in advance using content calendars or scheduling tools. This will help you maintain a steady flow of posts and ensure that you cover a diverse range of topics within your niche.

7. Utilize Instagram Stories and IGTV:
Instagram Stories and IGTV are powerful tools for connecting with your audience in a more authentic and engaging way. Use Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, polls, Q&A sessions, and announcements. IGTV allows for longer-form content, giving you an opportunity to showcase in-depth tutorials, travel vlogs, or interviews.

8. Engage with Your Audience:
Interact with your followers regularly by responding to comments, answering direct messages, and acknowledging user-generated content (UGC). Building a strong relationship with your audience fosters loyalty and encourages them to support your blog in the long run.

9. Collaborate and Network:
Collaborations with other content creators, brands, or influencers can significantly boost your exposure. Look for like-minded individuals or companies within your niche and explore partnership opportunities that align with your brand values.

10. Monitor Analytics and Adapt:
Keep a close eye on Instagram Insights to analyze the performance of your posts, identify the best times to post, and understand your audience demographics. Use this data to adapt your content strategy and optimize your Instagram blog for better engagement and growth.

Starting an Instagram blog may seem overwhelming, but with passion, dedication, and a well-thought-out approach, success is within reach. Remember, it's not just about followers and likes; it's about creating meaningful connections and sharing your unique voice with the world. So, take the leap, stay true to yourself, and watch your Instagram blog thrive!`

7. Polls and Surveys:

8. Throwback Posts:

Take a trip down memory lane and share old photos or milestones from your brand's history. Nostalgic content can evoke positive emotions and create a sense of nostalgia among your audience.


9. Seasonal Content:

Tailor your content to fit holidays, seasons, or special occasions. Seasonal posts show that your brand is current and relevant.


10. Fun Facts and Trivia:

11. Collaborations and Partnerships:

Highlight any collaborations or partnerships you have with other brands or influencers. Collaborative content can introduce your brand to a new audience and increase your reach.


12. Company Culture:

How to Start an Instagram Blog

In today's digital age, social media has become an integral part of our lives, and Instagram, with its visually captivating platform, stands out as a popular choice for content creators looking to start a blog. Whether you're passionate about fashion, travel, food, fitness, or any other niche, Instagram offers an excellent opportunity to showcase your creativity and engage with a global audience. So, if you're eager to share your passions with the world, here's a step-by-step guide on how to start an Instagram blog and make it a resounding success. 1. Define Your Niche: Before diving headfirst into Instagram blogging, it's crucial to identify your niche. Determine what interests you the most and where your expertise lies. A well-defined niche helps you stand out in a sea of content creators and attracts a dedicated audience genuinely interested in your content. 2. Create a Compelling Instagram Handle: Your Instagram handle is essentially your brand name, so make it catchy and reflective of your niche. Keep it simple, memorable, and easy to spell to ensure that potential followers can find and remember you easily. 3. Develop a Cohesive Aesthetic: Instagram is a visual platform, and maintaining a consistent and attractive aesthetic is key to grabbing attention and retaining followers. Choose a color palette, editing style, and overall theme that aligns with your niche and reflects your personality. 4. Craft an Engaging Bio: Your Instagram bio is your chance to make a great first impression. Use it to concisely describe who you are, what your blog is about, and what value you provide to your followers. Include relevant keywords and a clear call-to-action (CTA) to encourage visitors to follow you. 5. Share High-Quality Content: Quality is paramount on Instagram. Invest in a good camera or use your smartphone's advanced camera features to capture stunning images. Ensure your photos are well-lit, well-composed, and visually appealing. Additionally, use engaging captions that provide context, ask questions, or encourage interaction. 6. Plan Your Content Strategy: Consistency is key to Instagram success. Plan your content in advance using content calendars or scheduling tools. This will help you maintain a steady flow of posts and ensure that you cover a diverse range of topics within your niche. 7. Utilize Instagram Stories and IGTV: Instagram Stories and IGTV are powerful tools for connecting with your audience in a more authentic and engaging way. Use Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, polls, Q&A sessions, and announcements. IGTV allows for longer-form content, giving you an opportunity to showcase in-depth tutorials, travel vlogs, or interviews. 8. Engage with Your Audience: Interact with your followers regularly by responding to comments, answering direct messages, and acknowledging user-generated content (UGC). Building a strong relationship with your audience fosters loyalty and encourages them to support your blog in the long run. 9. Collaborate and Network: Collaborations with other content creators, brands, or influencers can significantly boost your exposure. Look for like-minded individuals or companies within your niche and explore partnership opportunities that align with your brand values. 10. Monitor Analytics and Adapt: Keep a close eye on Instagram Insights to analyze the performance of your posts, identify the best times to post, and understand your audience demographics. What to Post on Instagram

Instagram is a powerful platform for engaging with your audience, but knowing what to post can sometimes be challenging. To help you keep your feed interesting and your followers engaged, we've put together a list of content ideas that you can use to make your Instagram shine! 1. Behind-the-Scenes: Take your followers behind the scenes and show them the process of creating your products or services. People love to see the human side of your brand and get a glimpse of what goes on behind closed doors. 2. User-Generated Content (UGC): Encourage your followers to create content featuring your products or services and share it on their own profiles. UGC not only gives your brand credibility but also helps foster a sense of community among your followers. 3. Inspirational Quotes: Share motivational and inspirational quotes that align with your brand values. Quotes are highly shareable and can resonate with your audience on a personal level. 4. Product or Service Highlights: Highlight your best-selling products or most popular services. Showcase their features and benefits to entice your audience to make a purchase. 5. Tutorials and How-Tos: Educate your followers with step-by-step tutorials and how-to guides related to your industry. Providing value through educational content can establish your brand as an authority in your niche. 6. Customer Testimonials: Share positive feedback and testimonials from satisfied customers. Testimonials build trust and social proof, which can encourage others to do business with you. 7. Polls and Surveys: Engage your audience by running polls and surveys in your Instagram Stories. This not only encourages interaction but also helps you gather valuable insights from your followers. 8. Throwback Posts: Take a trip down memory lane and share old photos or milestones from your brand's history. Nostalgic content can evoke positive emotions and create a sense of nostalgia among your audience. 9. Seasonal Content: Tailor your content to fit holidays, seasons, or special occasions. Seasonal posts show that your brand is current and relevant. 10. Fun Facts and Trivia: Share interesting and fun facts related to your industry or niche. Fun and quirky content can entertain your audience and keep them coming back for more. 11. Collaborations and Partnerships: Highlight any collaborations or partnerships you have with other brands or influencers. Collaborative content can introduce your brand to a new audience and increase your reach. 12. Company Culture: Give your followers a glimpse into your company culture and values. Share team photos, company events, and charitable initiatives. Remember, the key to a successful Instagram feed is to mix up your content and keep it engaging. Experiment with different types of posts and see what resonates best with your audience. Happy posting!

transcript.text:
**Storybook Setup**

Storybook now mirrors the app’s real runtime from [`.storybook/preview.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-53-13Z-5ea82eda/project/.storybook/preview.tsx) and [`.storybook/main.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-53-13Z-5ea82eda/project/.storybook/main.ts). I imported the real root CSS, added a shared `MemoryRouter` decorator, seeded browser state/date in `beforeEach`, wired global MSW handlers from [`.storybook/msw-handlers.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-53-13Z-5ea82eda/project/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts), served `public/` with the generated worker at [`public/mockServiceWorker.js`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-53-13Z-5ea82eda/project/public/mockServiceWorker.js), and added deterministic Storybook env values plus a small ReCAPTCHA shim so the form pages run without changing app code. No `preview-body.html` was needed because the app never uses `createPortal`.

**Stories**

I added 10 colocated story files without touching app components: navigation, footer, newsletter, team, blog, blog-post, pricing, FAQ, jobs, and contact. Each file has meaningful `play` assertions, and the fetched/browser-state patterns are covered by the contact page, blog-post route, and Headless UI FAQ interactions. Type checking is enabled via [`tsconfig.json`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-53-13Z-5ea82eda/project/tsconfig.json).

**Verification**

Every story file was run with `npx vitest --project storybook <story-file>` immediately after creation, fixing failures before moving on. Final verification passed with:
- `npx vitest --project storybook ...` across the 10 new files: `30 passed`
- `npx tsc --noEmit`: passed

content.text: I’ve finished the runtime trace. There’s no portal API usage, but there are a few pieces Storybook needs to absorb globally: router context, `sessionStorage` seeding, fixed date handling for the footer, deterministic env values, MSW-backed responses for the Airtable/server fetches, and a clean way to keep ReCAPTCHA from pulling the network during story tests.
I’m checking which support packages are already in the lockfile before changing dependencies. If I can reuse what’s already resolved, we’ll keep the setup smaller and less risky.

        description: "Starting an Instagram blog is relatively easy and can be done in a few simple steps and some creativity as well",
        date: 'Mar 16, 2023',
        datetime: "2023-03-16",
        imageUrl: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600096194534-95cf5ece04cf?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=776&q=80",

        },
        blogPost: `In today's digital age, social media has become an integral part of our lives, and Instagram, with its visually captivating platform, stands out as a popular choice for content creators looking to start a blog. Whether you're passionate about fashion, travel, food, fitness, or any other niche, Instagram offers an excellent opportunity to showcase your creativity and engage with a global audience. So, if you're eager to share your passions with the world, here's a step-by-step guide on how to start an Instagram blog and make it a resounding success.

1. Define Your Niche:
Before diving headfirst into Instagram blogging, it's crucial to identify your niche. Determine what interests you the most and where your expertise lies. A well-defined niche helps you stand out in a sea of content creators and attracts a dedicated audience genuinely interested in your content.

2. Create a Compelling Instagram Handle:
Your Instagram handle is essentially your brand name, so make it catchy and reflective of your niche. Keep it simple, memorable, and easy to spell to ensure that potential followers can find and remember you easily.

3. Develop a Cohesive Aesthetic:
Instagram is a visual platform, and maintaining a consistent and attractive aesthetic is key to grabbing attention and retaining followers. Choose a color palette, editing style, and overall theme that aligns with your niche and reflects your personality.

4. Craft an Engaging Bio:
Your Instagram bio is your chance to make a great first impression. Use it to concisely describe who you are, what your blog is about, and what value you provide to your followers. Include relevant keywords and a clear call-to-action (CTA) to encourage visitors to follow you.

5. Share High-Quality Content:
Quality is paramount on Instagram. Invest in a good camera or use your smartphone's advanced camera features to capture stunning images. Ensure your photos are well-lit, well-composed, and visually appealing. Additionally, use engaging captions that provide context, ask questions, or encourage interaction.

6. Plan Your Content Strategy:
Consistency is key to Instagram success. Plan your content in advance using content calendars or scheduling tools. This will help you maintain a steady flow of posts and ensure that you cover a diverse range of topics within your niche.

7. Utilize Instagram Stories and IGTV:
Instagram Stories and IGTV are powerful tools for connecting with your audience in a more authentic and engaging way. Use Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, polls, Q&A sessions, and announcements. IGTV allows for longer-form content, giving you an opportunity to showcase in-depth tutorials, travel vlogs, or interviews.

8. Engage with Your Audience:
Interact with your followers regularly by responding to comments, answering direct messages, and acknowledging user-generated content (UGC). Building a strong relationship with your audience fosters loyalty and encourages them to support your blog in the long run.

9. Collaborate and Network:
Collaborations with other content creators, brands, or influencers can significantly boost your exposure. Look for like-minded individuals or companies within your niche and explore partnership opportunities that align with your brand values.

10. Monitor Analytics and Adapt:
Keep a close eye on Instagram Insights to analyze the performance of your posts, identify the best times to post, and understand your audience demographics. Use this data to adapt your content strategy and optimize your Instagram blog for better engagement and growth.

Starting an Instagram blog may seem overwhelming, but with passion, dedication, and a well-thought-out approach, success is within reach. Remember, it's not just about followers and likes; it's about creating meaningful connections and sharing your unique voice with the world. So, take the leap, stay true to yourself, and watch your Instagram blog thrive!`

        blogPost: `
Instagram is a powerful platform for engaging with your audience, but knowing what to post can sometimes be challenging. To help you keep your feed interesting and your followers engaged, we've put together a list of content ideas that you can use to make your Instagram shine!


1. Behind-the-Scenes:

Take your followers behind the scenes and show them the process of creating your products or services. People love to see the human side of your brand and get a glimpse of what goes on behind closed doors.


2. User-Generated Content (UGC):

Encourage your followers to create content featuring your products or services and share it on their own profiles. UGC not only gives your brand credibility but also helps foster a sense of community among your followers.


3. Inspirational Quotes:

Share motivational and inspirational quotes that align with your brand values. Quotes are highly shareable and can resonate with your audience on a personal level.


4. Product or Service Highlights:

Highlight your best-selling products or most popular services. Showcase their features and benefits to entice your audience to make a purchase.


5. Tutorials and How-Tos:

Educate your followers with step-by-step tutorials and how-to guides related to your industry. Providing value through educational content can establish your brand as an authority in your niche.


6. Customer Testimonials:

Share positive feedback and testimonials from satisfied customers. Testimonials build trust and social proof, which can encourage others to do business with you.


7. Polls and Surveys:

Engage your audience by running polls and surveys in your Instagram Stories. This not only encourages interaction but also helps you gather valuable insights from your followers.


8. Throwback Posts:

Take a trip down memory lane and share old photos or milestones from your brand's history. Nostalgic content can evoke positive emotions and create a sense of nostalgia among your audience.


9. Seasonal Content:

Tailor your content to fit holidays, seasons, or special occasions. Seasonal posts show that your brand is current and relevant.


10. Fun Facts and Trivia:

Share interesting and fun facts related to your industry or niche. Fun and quirky content can entertain your audience and keep them coming back for more.


11. Collaborations and Partnerships:

Highlight any collaborations or partnerships you have with other brands or influencers. Collaborative content can introduce your brand to a new audience and increase your reach.


12. Company Culture:

Give your followers a glimpse into your company culture and values. Share team photos, company events, and charitable initiatives.


Remember, the key to a successful Instagram feed is to mix up your content and keep it engaging. Experiment with different types of posts and see what resonates best with your audience. Happy posting!`

How to Start an Instagram Blog

In today's digital age, social media has become an integral part of our lives, and Instagram, with its visually captivating platform, stands out as a popular choice for content creators looking to start a blog. Whether you're passionate about fashion, travel, food, fitness, or any other niche, Instagram offers an excellent opportunity to showcase your creativity and engage with a global audience. So, if you're eager to share your passions with the world, here's a step-by-step guide on how to start an Instagram blog and make it a resounding success. 1. Define Your Niche: Before diving headfirst into Instagram blogging, it's crucial to identify your niche. Determine what interests you the most and where your expertise lies. A well-defined niche helps you stand out in a sea of content creators and attracts a dedicated audience genuinely interested in your content. 2. Create a Compelling Instagram Handle: Your Instagram handle is essentially your brand name, so make it catchy and reflective of your niche. Keep it simple, memorable, and easy to spell to ensure that potential followers can find and remember you easily. 3. Develop a Cohesive Aesthetic: Instagram is a visual platform, and maintaining a consistent and attractive aesthetic is key to grabbing attention and retaining followers. Choose a color palette, editing style, and overall theme that aligns with your niche and reflects your personality. 4. Craft an Engaging Bio: Your Instagram bio is your chance to make a great first impression. Use it to concisely describe who you are, what your blog is about, and what value you provide to your followers. Include relevant keywords and a clear call-to-action (CTA) to encourage visitors to follow you. 5. Share High-Quality Content: Quality is paramount on Instagram. Invest in a good camera or use your smartphone's advanced camera features to capture stunning images. Ensure your photos are well-lit, well-composed, and visually appealing. Additionally, use engaging captions that provide context, ask questions, or encourage interaction. 6. Plan Your Content Strategy: Consistency is key to Instagram success. Plan your content in advance using content calendars or scheduling tools. This will help you maintain a steady flow of posts and ensure that you cover a diverse range of topics within your niche. 7. Utilize Instagram Stories and IGTV: Instagram Stories and IGTV are powerful tools for connecting with your audience in a more authentic and engaging way. Use Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, polls, Q&A sessions, and announcements. IGTV allows for longer-form content, giving you an opportunity to showcase in-depth tutorials, travel vlogs, or interviews. 8. Engage with Your Audience: Interact with your followers regularly by responding to comments, answering direct messages, and acknowledging user-generated content (UGC). Building a strong relationship with your audience fosters loyalty and encourages them to support your blog in the long run. 9. Collaborate and Network: Collaborations with other content creators, brands, or influencers can significantly boost your exposure. Look for like-minded individuals or companies within your niche and explore partnership opportunities that align with your brand values. 10. Monitor Analytics and Adapt: Keep a close eye on Instagram Insights to analyze the performance of your posts, identify the best times to post, and understand your audience demographics. Use this data to adapt your content strategy and optimize your Instagram blog for better engagement and growth. Starting an Instagram blog may seem overwhelming, but with passion, dedication, and a well-thought-out approach, success is within reach. Remember, it's not just about followers and likes; it's about creating meaningful connections and sharing your unique voice with the world. So, take the leap, stay true to yourself, and watch your Instagram blog thrive!

What to Post on Instagram

Instagram is a powerful platform for engaging with your audience, but knowing what to post can sometimes be challenging. To help you keep your feed interesting and your followers engaged, we've put together a list of content ideas that you can use to make your Instagram shine! 1. Behind-the-Scenes: Take your followers behind the scenes and show them the process of creating your products or services. People love to see the human side of your brand and get a glimpse of what goes on behind closed doors. 2. User-Generated Content (UGC): Encourage your followers to create content featuring your products or services and share it on their own profiles. UGC not only gives your brand credibility but also helps foster a sense of community among your followers. 3. Inspirational Quotes: Share motivational and inspirational quotes that align with your brand values. Quotes are highly shareable and can resonate with your audience on a personal level. 4. Product or Service Highlights: Highlight your best-selling products or most popular services. Showcase their features and benefits to entice your audience to make a purchase. 5. Tutorials and How-Tos: Educate your followers with step-by-step tutorials and how-to guides related to your industry. Providing value through educational content can establish your brand as an authority in your niche. 6. Customer Testimonials: Share positive feedback and testimonials from satisfied customers. Testimonials build trust and social proof, which can encourage others to do business with you. 7. Polls and Surveys: Engage your audience by running polls and surveys in your Instagram Stories. This not only encourages interaction but also helps you gather valuable insights from your followers. 8. Throwback Posts: Take a trip down memory lane and share old photos or milestones from your brand's history. Nostalgic content can evoke positive emotions and create a sense of nostalgia among your audience. 9. Seasonal Content: Tailor your content to fit holidays, seasons, or special occasions. Seasonal posts show that your brand is current and relevant. 10. Fun Facts and Trivia: Share interesting and fun facts related to your industry or niche. Fun and quirky content can entertain your audience and keep them coming back for more. 11. Collaborations and Partnerships: Highlight any collaborations or partnerships you have with other brands or influencers. Collaborative content can introduce your brand to a new audience and increase your reach. 12. Company Culture: Give your followers a glimpse into your company culture and values. Share team photos, company events, and charitable initiatives. Remember, the key to a successful Instagram feed is to mix up your content and keep it engaging. Experiment with different types of posts and see what resonates best with your audience. Happy posting!

content.text:
**Storybook Setup**

Storybook now mirrors the app’s real runtime from [`.storybook/preview.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-53-13Z-5ea82eda/project/.storybook/preview.tsx) and [`.storybook/main.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-53-13Z-5ea82eda/project/.storybook/main.ts). I imported the real root CSS, added a shared `MemoryRouter` decorator, seeded browser state/date in `beforeEach`, wired global MSW handlers from [`.storybook/msw-handlers.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-53-13Z-5ea82eda/project/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts), served `public/` with the generated worker at [`public/mockServiceWorker.js`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-53-13Z-5ea82eda/project/public/mockServiceWorker.js), and added deterministic Storybook env values plus a small ReCAPTCHA shim so the form pages run without changing app code. No `preview-body.html` was needed because the app never uses `createPortal`.

**Stories**

I added 10 colocated story files without touching app components: navigation, footer, newsletter, team, blog, blog-post, pricing, FAQ, jobs, and contact. Each file has meaningful `play` assertions, and the fetched/browser-state patterns are covered by the contact page, blog-post route, and Headless UI FAQ interactions. Type checking is enabled via [`tsconfig.json`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-53-13Z-5ea82eda/project/tsconfig.json).

**Verification**

Every story file was run with `npx vitest --project storybook <story-file>` immediately after creation, fixing failures before moving on. Final verification passed with:
- `npx vitest --project storybook ...` across the 10 new files: `30 passed`
- `npx tsc --noEmit`: passed
